title: ChatGPT API Cheat Sheet
description: Essential guide for engineers using the ChatGPT API effectively.

commands.description: Controls randomness in the output; higher values for creative tasks.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Limits the response length to manage cost and relevance.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on API costs.

commands.description: Enables streaming responses for faster interaction.
commands.scenario: When building a real-time chat application that requires quick responses.

commands.description: Selects the GPT-4 model for more advanced reasoning.
commands.scenario: When developing an application that requires complex problem-solving capabilities.

commands.description: Instructs the model to summarize given text.
commands.scenario: When creating a tool to generate summaries of articles or documents.

commands.description: Reduces repetition by penalizing frequent tokens.
commands.scenario: When generating diverse content without repeating phrases.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics.
commands.scenario: When you want to explore a wide range of topics in a conversation.

commands.description: Defines stop sequences to control where the model stops generating text.
commands.scenario: When you need the model to stop generating after a certain point.

commands.description: Generates multiple completions to choose the best one.
commands.scenario: When you want to present multiple options to users for selection.

commands.description: Returns the log probabilities of the top tokens for analysis.
commands.scenario: When you need to analyze the model's decision-making process.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authenticating requests.
commands.scenario: When initializing the API client to start making requests.

commands.description: Enables deterministic outputs by setting a specific seed value, useful for debugging and consistent testing.
Forces the model to output a valid JSON object. Requires the word 'JSON' to be present in the system or user prompt.
Nucleus sampling: limits the model's vocabulary to a subset of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches the threshold P.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a specialized code reviewer."
commands.description: Defines the persona and behavioral constraints of the assistant, separating high-level instructions from user input.
Enables Function Calling, allowing the model to output structured arguments to call external APIs or local code.
